message:
feat(cancel): add drain phase classification and Freedman inequality bound to progress certificates

Progress certificates now track cancellation drain lifecycle via a
DrainPhase enum (Warmup → RapidDrain → SlowTail → Stalled → Quiescent)
determined automatically from the credit stream's exponential moving
average. This enables phase-adaptive timeout policies where rapid drain
uses aggressive timeouts and slow tail warrants patience.

CertificateVerdict gains a Freedman inequality bound that strictly
dominates Azuma-Hoeffding when empirical variance is below worst-case,
plus empirical variance of per-step deltas for richer diagnostics.

Symbol cancel broadcaster fixed to avoid holding the read lock on
active_tokens during token.cancel() calls — the token is now cloned
and the lock released before cancel executes, preventing potential
deadlock when cancel handlers need write access to the token map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
